# Strategy

## Goal

Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge
(`references/challenge.lean.ref`): the nine protected declarations headed
by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and `Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` —
an Albanese/Jacobian object uniform over the `k`-rational pointing of a
smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C/k`, with **no** `C(k)≠∅`
hypothesis and **no** `CharZero` (only `[Field k]`).

The witness `J` is always constructed unconditionally; only `isAlbaneseFor`
is quantified over the pointing `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. Spine: **pointed vs.
unpointed**. `genus C := dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` (protected; cannot be re-typed).

**Where the goal stands.** No protected decl yet closes with a kernel-only
`#print axioms`; each closes modulo the open Route-A cone plus the
Riemann–Roch (RR) substrate. The USER ROUTE C PAUSE freezes the RR files.
The operative posture is option (c): forward-compatible Route-A substrate
work while the unconditional contract waits on a RR disposition.

**RR dependency narrowed (iter-206, strategy-auditor validated).** Bare
`Pic_{C/k}` representability and the identity component `Pic^z` are
**RR-free** (Kleiman §4–§5, Nitsure §5). So the primary goal A.2.c no
longer transits RR. RR is now forced only at three downstream nodes:
genus formula `dim J = g`, autoduality `J ≅ J^∨`, and `Sym^d`
surjectivity (only if the `Sym^d`/Abel route is used). The Albanese UP
itself has an RR-free route (Kleiman `rmk:Alb`, output `J^∨`). This may let
the goal close without lifting the pause — see Open questions; surfaced to
USER.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A.1.c.SubT — line-bundle tensor group law | active (sole productive lane); **flat-pivot iter-206** | ~3–5 | ~80–150 · ~40/it | flat-exactness (`Module.Flat.lTensor…`, in Mathlib) | none structural — the absent-`MonoidalClosed` wall is bypassed |
| A.1.c — RelPic functor | held (placeholder bodies) | ~3–5 post-SubT | ~50–150 · 0/it | A.1.c.SubT; ét-sheafify on `Over S` | RE-ENGAGE GATE: replace dishonest `PicSharp := const PUnit` + `functorial := 0` first |
| A.4.a — codim-1 + Weil divisor | substrate DONE; terminal USER-blocked | ~0 | ~40 · 0/it | `[IsNoetherian X]`/`[CompactSpace X]` sig | sig strengthening is USER-gated |
| A.4.c.0 — codim-≥2 conclusion | **STUCK + PAUSED** pending USER | USER-gated | ~0/it | Stacks 02JK conormal iso (Step A2) | 27 iters flat; "one more input" receded 4× |
| A.2.c — FGA Pic representability | priority-2 (RR-free per iter-206) | ~12–16 | ~600–800 · 0/it | A.1.c + Quot/Cartier engine | large build; was wrongly thought RR-blocked |
| A.3.0/ii/vii — tangent + Pic⁰ AV-structure | gated on A.2.c (USER: no A.3 before A.2.c) | ~26–45 | ~1100–2100 · 0/it | scheme Hilbert poly; tangent iso | absent in Mathlib |
| A.4.c.1 / A.4.d — Thm 3.2 + Albanese UP | gated A.2.c (+ COE / RR) | ~19–33 | ~800–1500 · 0/it | divisor map; Cartier on `C×Pic^d` | RR at genus formula / autoduality |
| genusZero arm + `nonempty_jacobianWitness` body | gated A.3 | ~5–7 | ~250–450 · 0/it | tangent-iso + connectedness | hidden A.2.c transit |

**Total Route A**: ~140–220 iters / ~4000–7500 LOC.

## Routes

`J := Pic⁰_{C/k}` (Kleiman §4–§5, Nitsure §5, Milne III §6). Bottom-up
execution (USER directive): prover capacity flows to ungated roots; never a
gated target before its roots close; no A.3+ before A.2.c closes. Default
Route-A prover mode `mathlib-build`; every directive cites
Kleiman/Nitsure/Milne/Mumford/Hartshorne/Stacks/Matsumura/Atiyah–Macdonald.

**Critical path to A.2.c**: A.1.c.SubT (group law) → A.1.c (RelPic functor)
→ A.2.c (representability). All three are RR-free.

**A.1.c.SubT — line-bundle group law (flat-pivot, iter-206).** The group
law on the relative Picard functor is the group of iso-classes of LINE
BUNDLES under tensor — a family of *propositions* (`Nonempty (… ≅ …)`)
needing four existence-of-iso facts (assoc, unit, commutativity, inverse),
NOT a monoidal-category instance. Mirrors Mathlib's
`CommRing.Pic = Units (Skeleton …)` / `Module.Invertible`
(`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`); `IsLocallyTrivial` already matches that
Prop carrier. The one hard ingredient `tensorObj_restrict_iso` is elementary
flat-exactness for line bundles (flat ⇒ `lTensor` preserves injectivity).
The earlier full-`MonoidalCategory`-on-all-modules route — which bottomed
out in the verified-absent `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` — is
**ABANDONED** as over-building (`analogies/ts-design206.md`).

**A.2.c — representability is RR-free.** Validated against Nitsure §5 +

encodes A.2.c as 6 Prop-valued typeclasses with `⟨sorry⟩` constructors;

above, not at representability.

`J := Spec k` via Mumford rigidity (`C_{k̄} ≅ ℙ¹`, `Mor(ℙ¹,A)` constant,
descend) — substrate partially built, PAUSED, ~300–500 LOC under a USER
amendment.

  which needs RR (Milne p.88). The auditor's RR-free route instead defines
  `Pic⁰ := Pic^z` (identity component, Kleiman §5). Trade-off: `degComp`
  needs only Pphifin but is RR-dependent; `Pic^z` is RR-free but needs
  identity-component / clopen-descent infra (~350 LOC, the reason it was
  excised iter-197). Pick when A.2.c nears; not actionable now (downstream,
  gated). Surfaced to USER.

- Connected étale group scheme over a field, dim 0 = `Spec k` (~50–100).

**New project material.** AbelianVarietyRigidity, RigidityLemma,
Genus0BaseObjects/*, RiemannRoch/* (paused), Picard/{RelativeSpec,
LineBundlePullback, RelPicFunctor, FGAPicRepresentability, IdentityComponent,
Pic0AbelianVariety, QuotScheme, FlatteningStratification, TensorObjSubstrate},

CoheightBridge, AlbaneseUP}.

# Strategy Critic Directive

## Slug
pivot206

## What to read (and ONLY this)
- `.archon/STRATEGY.md` — read it verbatim; it is the object under review.
- `references/summary.md` — the reference index (below, also on disk).
- The blueprint chapter title list below (one line per chapter).
- The project goal paragraph below.

Do NOT read: iter sidecars (`.archon/iter/**`), `task_pending.md`,
`task_done.md`, prover task results, review reports, or any per-iter
narrative. Your value is a fresh view of the strategy with no sunk-cost
exposure.

## Project goal (verbatim from the challenge)
Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge: nine protected Lean
declarations headed by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — the existence of an Albanese/Jacobian
object uniform over the `k`-rational pointing of a smooth proper
geometrically irreducible curve `C/k`, with no `C(k) ≠ ∅` hypothesis and no
`CharZero` (only `[Field k]`). The witness `J` is constructed
unconditionally; only `isAlbaneseFor` is quantified over the pointing.

## Blueprint chapter index (title per chapter)
- AbelJacobi: The Abel–Jacobi map
- AbelianVarietyRigidity: morphisms from a genus-0 curve into an AV are constant
- Albanese_AlbaneseUP: Albanese universal property of Pic⁰_{C/k}
- Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum: Auslander–Buchsbaum
- Albanese_CodimOneExtension: codim-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
- Albanese_CoheightBridge: coheight–Krull-dim bridge
- Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension: Milne Thm 3.2 rational maps into AVs
- AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj: cotangent space at the identity (A.3.0)
- Cohomology_* (MayerVietoris, SheafCompose, StructureSheafAb, StructureSheafModuleK): sheaf-cohomology substrate
- Differentials: relative cotangent presheaf
- Genus: genus of a smooth proper curve
- Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate: Cross01 substrate
- Jacobian: the Jacobian as an abelian variety
- Picard_FGAPicRepresentability: FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)
- Picard_FlatteningStratification: flattening stratification
- Picard_IdentityComponent / Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety: identity component Pic⁰
- Picard_LineBundlePullback: line-bundle pullback on a relative curve
- Picard_QuotScheme: the Quot scheme
- Picard_RelPicFunctor: relative Picard functor + étale sheafification (A.1.c)
- Picard_RelativeSpec: relative Spec
- Picard_TensorObjSubstrate: line-bundle tensor group-law substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
- RiemannRoch_* (WeilDivisor, RRFormula, OCofP, OcOfD, RationalCurveIso, H1Vanishing): Riemann–Roch chain (PAUSED)
- Rigidity / RigidityKbar: rigidity for morphisms / over a base field

## references/summary.md (index)
(Read the file on disk for the full table; key sources: Kleiman "The Picard
scheme" §4 existence / §5 Pic⁰ / §6; Nitsure "Hilbert and Quot Schemes" §5;
Milne "Abelian Varieties" III §1/§4/§6; Mumford "Abelian Varieties"; Stacks
01CR Picard group; Mathlib `RingTheory.PicardGroup`.)

## Focus this iter
Two strategic changes landed this iter — assess both, plus the usual
soundness + canonical-skeleton + format audit:

1. **A.1.c.SubT flat/line-bundle pivot.** The relative Picard group law was
   re-scoped off a full `MonoidalCategory (Scheme.Modules X)` (which bottomed
   out in a verified-absent Mathlib `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`)
   onto the group of iso-classes of line bundles (four existence-of-iso
   lemmas + a flat-scoped restriction iso), mirroring Mathlib's
   `CommRing.Pic = Units (Skeleton …)` / `Module.Invertible`. Is this the
   right shape? Any risk the lighter route fails to deliver the full
   `AddCommGroup` the downstream RelPic functor needs?

2. **RR-free A.2.c.** A reference audit concluded bare `Pic_{C/k}`
   representability and the identity component `Pic^z` are RR-free (Kleiman
   §4–§5, Nitsure §5), narrowing the Route-C (Riemann–Roch, USER-paused)
   dependency to three downstream nodes only (genus formula `dim J = g`,
   autoduality `J ≅ J^∨`, `Sym^d` surjectivity if used). The STRATEGY's TOP
   open question is now whether to define `Pic⁰ := degComp` (RR-dependent,
   no identity-component infra) or `Pic⁰ := Pic^z` (RR-free, needs ~350 LOC
   identity-component / clopen-descent infra). Is the strategy's handling of
   this trade-off sound? Is leaving it as an open question (rather than
   deciding now) defensible given it is downstream and gated?

Render SOUND / CHALLENGE / REJECT per the usual rubric, plus the
canonical-skeleton + ≤250-line/≤12KB format check.

# Strategy

## Goal

Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge
(`references/challenge.lean.ref`): the nine protected declarations headed
by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and `Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` —
an Albanese/Jacobian object uniform over the `k`-rational pointing of a
smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C/k`, with **no** `C(k)≠∅`
hypothesis and **no** `CharZero` (only `[Field k]`).

The witness `J` is constructed unconditionally; only `isAlbaneseFor` is
quantified over the pointing `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`. Spine: **pointed vs.
unpointed**. `genus C := dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` (protected; cannot be re-typed).

**Where the goal stands.** No protected decl yet closes with a kernel-only
`#print axioms`; each closes modulo the open Route-A cone plus the
Riemann–Roch (RR) substrate, which the USER ROUTE C PAUSE freezes. The
operative posture is option (c): forward-compatible Route-A substrate work
while the unconditional contract waits on a RR disposition.

**RR enters the genus ≥ 1 path at few points.** Bare `Pic_{C/k}`
representability is RR-free (Kleiman §4, Nitsure §5). RR is forced only at:
the genus formula `dim J = g`; autoduality `J ≅ J^∨`; and `Sym^d`
surjectivity (only if the `Sym^d`/Abel route is used). **Caveat:** the
current witness `Pic⁰ := PicScheme.degComp C 0` identifies its component by
*degree*, which itself transits RR (Milne III §1); a fully RR-free witness
requires redefining `Pic⁰ := Pic^z` (identity component, Kleiman §5) — an
open choice (see Open questions), not yet purchased. So RR-freeness of the
*witness* is contingent; only representability is unconditionally RR-free.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A.1.c.SubT — line-bundle tensor group law | active; sole productive lane | ~3–5 | ~80–150 · ~40/it | flat-exactness (`Module.Flat.lTensor…`, present) | none structural — the absent-`MonoidalClosed` wall is bypassed |
| A.1.c — RelPic functor | held (placeholder bodies) | ~3–5 post-SubT | ~50–150 · 0/it | A.1.c.SubT; ét-sheafify on `Over S` | RE-ENGAGE GATE: replace dishonest `PicSharp := const PUnit` + `functorial := 0` first |
| A.2.c — FGA Pic representability | priority-2; RR-free | ~12–16 | ~600–800 · 0/it | A.1.c + Quot/Cartier engine | large build |
| A.4.c.0 — codim-≥2 conclusion | **EXCISION-PENDING** (see Routes) | route choice | ~120 · 0/it | Stacks 02JK conormal iso (Step A2) | 27 iters flat; likely obsoleted by the `rmk:Alb` UP route |
| A.3.0/ii/vii — tangent + Pic⁰ AV-structure | gated A.2.c (no A.3 before A.2.c) | ~26–45 | ~1100–2100 · 0/it | scheme tangent space; Hilbert poly | absent in Mathlib; likely under-counted |
| A.4 — Albanese UP | gated A.2.c; route under review | ~12–25 | ~600–1200 · 0/it | `rmk:Alb` (representability) OR Thm 3.2 cone | route choice decides which substrate is live |
| genusZero arm + `nonempty_jacobianWitness` body | gated A.3 | ~5–7 | ~250–450 · 0/it | tangent-iso + connectedness | hidden A.2.c transit |

**Total Route A**: ~120–180 iters / ~3500–6500 LOC (narrower if the
`rmk:Alb` UP route excises the Thm-3.2 cone).

## Routes

`J := Pic⁰_{C/k}` (Kleiman §4–§5, Nitsure §5, Milne III §6). Bottom-up
execution (USER directive): prover capacity flows to ungated roots; never a
gated target before its roots close; no A.3+ before A.2.c closes. Default
Route-A prover mode `mathlib-build`; every directive cites
Kleiman/Nitsure/Milne/Mumford/Hartshorne/Stacks/Matsumura/Atiyah–Macdonald.

**Critical path to A.2.c** (all RR-free): A.1.c.SubT (group law) → A.1.c
(RelPic functor) → A.2.c (representability).

**A.1.c.SubT — line-bundle group law.** The group law on the relative
Picard functor is the group of iso-classes of LINE BUNDLES under tensor — a
family of *propositions* (`Nonempty (… ≅ …)`) needing four
existence-of-iso facts (assoc, unit, commutativity, inverse), NOT a
monoidal-category instance. Mirrors Mathlib's
`CommRing.Pic = Units (Skeleton …)` / `Module.Invertible`
(`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`); `IsLocallyTrivial` matches that Prop
carrier. The one hard ingredient `tensorObj_restrict_iso` is elementary
flat-exactness for line bundles (flat ⇒ `lTensor` preserves injectivity;
`⊗` right-exact). The full `MonoidalCategory`-on-all-modules route (which
bottoms out in the absent `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`) is not
needed; rationale in `analogies/ts-design206.md`.

**A.2.c — representability is RR-free** (Nitsure §5 + Kleiman §4: zero RR in
the Quot construction). `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` encodes A.2.c as 6
Prop-valued typeclasses with `⟨sorry⟩` constructors; downstream Route A
proceeds under these. Carrier-soundness confirmed.

**Albanese UP — two routes, choice pending.** (1) **Milne Thm 3.2**: extend
the Abel–Jacobi rational map across `C`; needs the codim-1 (A.4.a) +
codim-≥2 (A.4.c.0, Stacks 02JK) + Thm-3.2-assembly cone. (2) **Kleiman
`rmk:Alb`**: the UP falls out of A.2.c representability + the Picard
functor's definition, RR-free, output `J^∨`; identifying `J^∨ ≅ J`
(autoduality) is a separate step that may need RR. Route (2), if it
delivers, **obsoletes the entire Thm-3.2 cone — including the 27-iter-stuck
A.4.c.0 / Stacks 02JK node, which would then be excised, not paused.** This
choice must be settled (strategy-auditor on `rmk:Alb` vs Thm 3.2) before any
further A.4 substrate spend.

**Route C — Riemann–Roch — PAUSED (USER directive).** RR files stay
imported, carrying inline sorries satisfied modulo option (c); paused-file
list in `task_pending.md`. Needed only at the three nodes named in Goal.

**Genus-0 arm.** (a) Route-A Pic⁰-via-AV-wrap (transits A.2.c); (b) direct
`J := Spec k` via Mumford rigidity (`C_{k̄} ≅ ℙ¹`, `Mor(ℙ¹,A)` constant,
descend) — substrate partially built, PAUSED, ~300–500 LOC under a USER
amendment.

## Open strategic questions

- **Albanese UP route: `rmk:Alb` vs Milne Thm 3.2 (TOP).** If `rmk:Alb`
  supplies the UP from representability, the Thm-3.2 cone (A.4.a, A.4.c.0 /
  02JK, A.4.c.1) is dead substrate. Decide on merits before A.4 spend;
  resolving it excises the stuck 02JK node. Surfaced to USER.
- **`Pic⁰` definition: `degComp` vs `Pic^z`.** `degComp` needs only Pphifin
  but identifies the component by degree (RR-dependent); `Pic^z` is RR-free
  but needs identity-component / clopen-descent infra (~350 LOC). A fully
  RR-free witness requires `Pic^z`. Pick when A.2.c nears; downstream/gated.
- **USER goal-amendment** (cheapest→costliest): (a) carve Rigidity out of
  the pause → genus-0 closure; (b) full Route C → unconditional end-state;
  (c) keep option-(c) holding pattern (current).
- **Genus-0 may need only `AbelianVarietyRigidity`, not `RigidityKbar`** if
  the target is `Spec k` (descent trivial). Audit vs Milne §1.
- **Lane COE STUCK pending USER**: residual = Stacks 02JK conormal iso. USER
  picks (a) 02JK Lean path, (b) 02JK-free pivot, or (c) axiom-guard the 3
  pinned decls + proceed downstream. (Likely mooted by the `rmk:Alb` route.)

## Mathlib gaps & new material

**Gaps to fill (Route A).**

- A.1.c: ét-sheafify on `Over S`; `LineBundle.OnProduct` carrier (~50–150).
- A.2.c: FGA Pic representability via Quot/Cartier (~600–800).
- A.3.0/ii/vii: scheme tangent space; Hilbert-poly decomposition + Pphifin;
  Pic⁰ AV-structure (~1100–2100).
- A.4 (route-dependent): `rmk:Alb` UP from representability, OR Thm-3.2 +
  codim-extension cone + Stacks 02JK conormal iso.
- Connected étale group scheme over a field, dim 0 = `Spec k` (~50–100).

**New project material.** AbelianVarietyRigidity, RigidityLemma,
Genus0BaseObjects/*, RiemannRoch/* (paused), Picard/{RelativeSpec,
LineBundlePullback, RelPicFunctor, FGAPicRepresentability, IdentityComponent,
Pic0AbelianVariety, QuotScheme, FlatteningStratification, TensorObjSubstrate},
Albanese/{AuslanderBuchsbaum, Thm32RationalMapExtension, CodimOneExtension,
CoheightBridge, AlbaneseUP}.

   flat-exactness (present in Mathlib), mirroring `CommRing.Pic`. Chapter
   rewritten + cleaned + HARD-GATE-cleared; TS prover dispatched on the
   pivot THIS iter.

   representability is RR-free; the stuck 02JK / Thm-3.2 cone is likely
   obsoleted by the Kleiman `rmk:Alb` Albanese-UP route.

  (now moot; that theorem was demoted this iter). Archived.
- iter-205 task_results archived to `task_results/archive/iter-205/`.

#1): (a) multi-file `MonoidalClosed` sub-lane; (b) flat-scoped line-bundle
pivot; (c) pause TS.

comparison-map construction `(L⊗M)|_f → L|_f ⊗ M|_f` cannot be built at the

# Iter-206 objectives (per-lane detail + reference citations)

## Lane TS — `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` [prove]

**Goal**: implement the flat/line-bundle group-law pivot (off the abandoned
all-modules `MonoidalCategory`/`MonoidalClosed` route).

**Reference anchors** (USER reference-driven directive):
- **Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §2** (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
  Defs. df:aPf + df:Pfs — the relative Picard functor is the
  abelian-group-valued quotient `Pic(C×_kT)/π_T^*Pic(T)`. Already cited
  verbatim in the chapter.
- **Stacks tag 01CR** — Picard group of a scheme = iso-classes of invertible
  sheaves under tensor (the group law).
- **Mathlib `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`**: `CommRing.Pic`,
  `instCommGroupPic`, `Module.Invertible`,
  `Module.Invertible.lTensor_bijective_iff` — the `Units (Skeleton …)`
  iso-class group idiom the pivot mirrors. (VERIFIED, ts-design206.)
- **Mathlib `Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic`**:
  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` — the flat-exactness
  ingredient behind `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. (VERIFIED.)
- `analogies/ts-design206.md` — the design rationale (primary).

**Ordered targets**:
1. `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (L249) — PRIMARY, the one hard ingredient.
   (a) construct the comparison map `(L⊗_X M)|_f → L|_f ⊗ M|_f` at the sheaf
   level; (b) prove iso via flat-exactness (line bundles flat via
   `Module.Invertible ⇒ Projective ⇒ Flat`).
2. `tensorObj_assoc_iso`, `tensorObj_unit_iso`, `tensorObj_comm_iso` — new,
   existence-of-iso on line bundles from (1) on a common trivialising cover.
3. `exists_tensorObj_inverse` (L300) — dual + contraction iso (Stacks 01CR).
4. Remove dead `monoidalCategory := sorry` (L150) + `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`
   + `monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW` (off-path).
5. `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (L339) — terminal cascade via QuotientAddGroup.

**HARD BAR**: re-scope + close `tensorObj_restrict_iso` axiom-clean + remove
the dead `monoidalCategory` instance. **Stretch**: the full cascade.

**Blueprint**: `chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (rewritten this iter;
HARD GATE cleared, tsgate206).

## Not dispatched (rationale)

- **COE** (`CodimOneExtension.lean`): PAUSED + EXCISION-PENDING (Stacks 02JK;
  likely obsoleted by the `rmk:Alb` Albanese-UP route). USER-gated.
- **RPF** (`RelPicFunctor.lean`): HELD; gated on TS `addCommGroup`; must-fix
  placeholder-replacement at re-engagement.
- **FGA / T32 / WD / RCI**: held/gated (see PROGRESS held-lanes).
- **A.3.* lanes**: forbidden before A.2.c (USER directive #4).
- **Route C**: PAUSED (USER directive #1).

## Route A — active prover lane

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` — **4 sorries** +
  the named ingredient `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (L249). A.1.c.SubT —
  **priority-1 (sole productive Route A lane)**; bottom-up spine
  TS→RPF→A.2.c. **iter-206 PIVOT**: the all-modules
  `MonoidalCategory`/`MonoidalClosed` route is ABANDONED (over-building;
  `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` verified absent). The group law is
  the group of iso-classes of LINE BUNDLES (propositions, no coherence),
  mirroring Mathlib `CommRing.Pic = Units (Skeleton …)` / `Module.Invertible`
  (`analogies/ts-design206.md`). One hard ingredient `tensorObj_restrict_iso`
  = elementary flat-exactness (`Module.Flat.lTensor…`, present). Targets:
  re-scope+close `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (construct comparison map + iso via
  flatness); add assoc/unit/comm iso lemmas; close `exists_tensorObj_inverse`;
  REMOVE dead `monoidalCategory := sorry` (L150) + the two transport lemmas;
  cascade to `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (L339).
  Blueprint: `chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (rewritten iter-206;
  HARD GATE cleared, tsgate206).

## Route A — PAUSED pending USER + EXCISION-PENDING

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` — **3 sorries**
  (L1262 `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`; L1459; L1534). A.4.c.0 —
  **STUCK + PAUSED** (27 iters flat on Stacks 02JK conormal iso, Step A2).
  **NOW EXCISION-PENDING**: the Kleiman `rmk:Alb` Albanese-UP route (RR-free,
  from representability) may obsolete the entire Thm-3.2 / codim-extension
  cone, excising this node. Decide the Albanese route (iter-207
  strategy-auditor on `rmk:Alb` vs Milne Thm 3.2) before any A.4 spend.
  No autonomous dispatch.

## Route A — held lanes (concrete re-engagement triggers)

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` — **0 sorries**,
  CLOSED iter-202. Substrate for the codim-extension cone — excision-pending
  with it.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` — **3 sorries**. A.4.a
  substrate DONE; terminal finite-support gated on USER-blocked
  `[IsNoetherian X]`/`[CompactSpace X]` sig. HELD.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` — **1 honest sorry** (L235
  `addCommGroup`) + 5 placeholder-body sorries. A.1.c. HELD; re-opens post
  TS `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`. **RE-ENGAGEMENT GATE / must-fix**: replace
  dishonest `PicSharp := const PUnit` (L330) + `functorial := 0` (L377) with
  the real `Units (Skeleton …)`-shaped construction FIRST.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean` — **7 sorries**
  (`⟨sorry⟩` instance constructors). A.2.c — RR-free (build via Quot/Cartier).
  HELD; re-opens post Lane RPF.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` — **2 sorries**.
  A.4.c.1. HELD + EXCISION-PENDING (`rmk:Alb` route may obsolete).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean` — Route C PAUSED + HELD.

## Route C — PAUSED (USER standing directive)

H1Vanishing, RRFormula, OCofP, OcOfD, RationalCurveIso, BareScheme,
GmScaling, RigidityKbar, AbelianVarietyRigidity — all OFF-LIMITS.
End-state satisfied modulo these per option (c).

## Iter-206 summary

This was a strategic-inflection iter: both productive Route-A lanes (TS, COE) had bottomed out in **absent multi-file Mathlib infrastructure**. Rather than open a Mathlib-PR-scale sub-lane, I ran three read-only consults and turned the inflection into decisive moves.

**Consults**
- **progress-critic route206** → TS **CHURNING** (3 net-0-ish iters; receding-horizon blocker); must commit a fork this iter.
- **mathlib-analogist ts-design206** (api-alignment) → **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (critical): the full `MonoidalCategory`/`MonoidalClosed`-on-all-modules route is **over-building**. The Pic group law is the group of iso-classes of line bundles (propositions, no coherence), mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic = Units (Skeleton …)`; the one hard ingredient reduces to **elementary flat-exactness** — present in Mathlib, not the absent `MonoidalClosed`.
- **strategy-auditor rrfree206** → bare `Pic_{C/k}` representability + `Pic^z` are **RR-free** (Kleiman §4–§5, Nitsure §5).

**Decisions made**
1. **TS fork → lightweight flat/line-bundle pivot** (committed; not the MonoidalClosed sub-lane). Chapter rewritten (writer + clean) and **HARD-GATE-cleared** (scoped reviewer); TS prover dispatched on the pivot **this iter** — onto Mathlib-present footing, ending the churn.
2. **02JK / Thm-3.2 cone → EXCISION-PENDING**: the Kleiman `rmk:Alb` Albanese-UP route may obsolete the 27-iter-stuck node entirely; committed an iter-207 strategy-auditor to decide the Albanese route before any A.4 spend.

**User hint serviced** — STRATEGY.md rewritten cleaner (timeless prose, per-iter narrative stripped per strategy-critic DRIFTED, RR-free framing reconciled so it no longer over-claims).

**Dispatch this iter**: 1 lane — `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (`prove`, flat-pivot). COE paused/excision-pending; RPF/FGA/T32/WD/RCI held; no A.3 (USER directive). Surfaced to USER (via the sidecar→TO_USER channel): A.2.c is RR-free, and the stuck 02JK node is likely excisable via `rmk:Alb`.
